= 0; i < filters.size(); i++) {\n227:     const auto& proto_config = filters[i];\n228:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"  filter #{}:\", i);\n229:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"    name: {}\", proto_config.name());\n230:     // dynamic listener filter configuration\n231:     if (proto_config.config_type_case() ==\n232:         envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter::ConfigTypeCase::kConfigDiscovery) {\n233:       const envoy::config::core::v3::ExtensionConfigSource& config_discovery =\n234:           proto_config.config_discovery();\n235:       const std::string& name = proto_config.name();\n236:       if (config_discovery.apply_default_config_without_warming() &&\n237:           !config_discovery.has_default_config()) {\n238:         return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n239:             \"Error: listener filter config {} applied without warming but has no default config.\",\n240:             name));\n241:       }\n242:       for (absl::string_view type_url : config_discovery.type_urls()) {\n243:         absl::string_view factory_type_url = TypeUtil::typeUrlToDescriptorFullName(type_url);\n244:         if (Registry::FactoryRegistry<Server::Configuration::NamedQuicListenerFilterConfigFactory>::\n245:                 getFactoryByType(factory_type_url) == nullptr) {\n246:           return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n247:               \"Error: no listener factory found for a required type URL {}.\", factory_type_url));\n248:         }\n249:       }\n250:       ret.push_back(config_provider_manager.createDynamicFilterConfigProvider(\n251:           config_discovery, name, context.serverFactoryContext(), context,\n252:           context.serverFactoryContext().clusterManager(), false, \"quic-listener\",\n253:           createListenerFilterMatcher(proto_config)));\n254:     } else {\n255:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"  config: {}\",\n256:                 MessageUtil::convertToStringForLogs(\n257:                     static_cast<const Protobuf::Message&>(proto_config.typed_config())));\n258:       // For static configuration, now see if there is a factory that will accept the config.\n259:       auto& factory =\n260:           Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactory<Configuration::NamedQuicListenerFilterConfigFactory>(\n261:               proto_config);\n262:       const auto message = Config::Utility::translateToFactoryConfig(\n263:           proto_config, context.messageValidationVisitor(), factory);\n264:       const auto callback = factory.createListenerFilterFactoryFromProto(\n265:           *message, createListenerFilterMatcher(proto_config), context);\n266:       ret.push_back(\n267:           config_provider_manager.createStaticFilterConfigProvider(callback, proto_config.name()));\n268:     }\n269:   }\n270:   return ret;\n271: }\n272: \n273: Network::ListenerFilterMatcherSharedPtr ProdListenerComponentFactory::createListenerFilterMatcher(\n274:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter& listener_filter) {\n275:   if (!listener_filter.has_filter_disabled()) {\n276:     return nullptr;\n277:   }\n278:   return {Network::ListenerFilterMatcherBuilder::buildListenerFilterMatcher(\n279:       listener_filter.filter_disabled())};\n280: }\n281: \n282: absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> ProdListenerComponentFactory::createListenSocket(\n283:     Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address, Network::Socket::Type socket_type,\n284:     const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options, BindType bind_type,\n285:     const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options, uint32_t worker_index) {\n286:   ASSERT(socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream ||\n287:          socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Datagram);\n288: \n289:   // First we try to get the socket from our parent if applicable in each case below.\n290:   if (address->type() == Network::Address::Type::Pipe) {\n291:     if (socket_type != Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n292:       // This could be implemented in the future, since Unix domain sockets\n293:       // support SOCK_DGRAM, but there would need to be a way to specify it in\n294:       // envoy.api.v2.core.Pipe.\n295:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n296:           fmt::format(\"socket type {} not supported for pipes\", toString(socket_type)));\n297:     }\n298:     const std::string addr = fmt::format(\"unix://{}\", address->asString());\n299:     const int fd = server_.hotRestart().duplicateParentListenSocket(addr, worker_index);\n300:     Network::IoHandlePtr io_handle = std::make_unique<Network::IoSocketHandleImpl>(fd);\n301:     if (io_handle->isOpen()) {\n302:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"obtained socket for address {} from parent\", addr);\n303:       return std::make_shared<Network::UdsListenSocket>(std::move(io_handle), address);\n304:     }\n305:     return std::make_shared<Network::UdsListenSocket>(address);\n306:   } else if (address->type() == Network::Address::Type::EnvoyInternal) {\n307:     // Listener manager should have validated that envoy internal address doesn't work with udp\n308:     // listener yet.\n309:     ASSERT(socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream);\n310:     return std::make_shared<Network::InternalListenSocket>(address);\n311:   }\n312: \n313:   const std::string scheme = (socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream)\n314:                                  ? std::string(Network::Utility::TCP_SCHEME)\n315:                                  : std::string(Network::Utility::UDP_SCHEME);\n316:   const std::string addr = absl::StrCat(scheme, address->asString());\n317: \n318:   if (bind_type != BindType::NoBind) {\n319:     const int fd = server_.hotRestart().duplicateParentListenSocket(addr, worker_index);\n320:     if (fd != -1) {\n321:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"obtained socket for address {} from parent\", addr);\n322:       Network::IoHandlePtr io_handle = std::make_unique<Network::IoSocketHandleImpl>(fd);\n323:       if (socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n324:         return std::make_shared<Network::TcpListenSocket>(std::move(io_handle), address, options);\n325:       } else {\n326:         auto socket = std::make_shared<Network::UdpListenSocket>(\n327:             std::move(io_handle), address, options,\n328:             server_.hotRestart().parentDrainedCallbackRegistrar());\n329:         return socket;\n330:       }\n331:     }\n332:   }\n333: \n334:   if (socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n335:     return std::make_shared<Network::TcpListenSocket>(\n336:         address, options, bind_type != BindType::NoBind, creation_options);\n337:   } else {\n338:     return std::make_shared<Network::UdpListenSocket>(\n339:         address, options, bind_type != BindType::NoBind, creation_options);\n340:   }\n341: }\n342: \n343: DrainManagerPtr ProdListenerComponentFactory::createDrainManager(\n344:     envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener::DrainType drain_type) {\n345:   return DrainManagerPtr{new DrainManagerImpl(server_, drain_type)};\n346: }\n347: \n348: DrainingFilterChainsManager::DrainingFilterChainsManager(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\n349:                                                          uint64_t workers_pending_removal)\n350:     : draining_listener_(std::move(draining_listener)),\n351:       workers_pending_removal_(workers_pending_removal) {}\n352: \n353: ListenerManagerImpl::ListenerManagerImpl(Instance& server,\n354:                                          std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory>&& factory,\n355:                                          WorkerFactory& worker_factory,\n356:                                          bool enable_dispatcher_stats,\n357:                                          Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names)\n358:     : server_(server), factory_(std::move(factory)),\n359:       scope_(server.stats().createScope(\"listener_manager.\")), stats_(generateStats(*scope_)),\n360:       enable_dispatcher_stats_(enable_dispatcher_stats), quic_stat_names_(quic_stat_names) {\n361:   if (!factory_) {\n362:     factory_ = std::make_unique<ProdListenerComponentFactory>(server);\n363:   }\n364:   if (server.admin().has_value()) {\n365:     config_tracker_entry_ = server.admin()->getConfigTracker().add(\n366:         \"listeners\", [this](const Matchers::StringMatcher& name_matcher) {\n367:           return dumpListenerConfigs(name_matcher);\n368:         });\n369:   }\n370: \n371:   for (uint32_t i = 0; i < server.options().concurrency(); i++) {\n372:     workers_.emplace_back(worker_factory.createWorker(\n373:         i, server.overloadManager(), server.nullOverloadManager(), absl::StrCat(\"worker_\", i)));\n374:   }\n375: }\n376: \n377: ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr\n378: ListenerManagerImpl::dumpListenerConfigs(const Matchers::StringMatcher& name_matcher) {\n379:   auto config_dump = std::make_unique<envoy::admin::v3::ListenersConfigDump>();\n380:   config_dump->set_version_info(lds_api_ != nullptr ? lds_api_->versionInfo() : \"\");\n381: \n382:   using DynamicListener = envoy::admin::v3::ListenersConfigDump::DynamicListener;\n383:   using DynamicListenerState = envoy::admin::v3::ListenersConfigDump::DynamicListenerState;\n384:   absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, DynamicListener*> listener_map;\n385: \n386:   for (const auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n387:     if (!name_matcher.match(listener->config().name())) {\n388:       continue;\n389:     }\n390:     if (listener->blockRemove()) {\n391:       auto& static_listener = *config_dump->mutable_static_listeners()->Add();\n392:       static_listener.mutable_listener()->PackFrom(listener->config());\n393:       TimestampUtil::systemClockToTimestamp(listener->last_updated_,\n394:                                             *(static_listener.mutable_last_updated()));\n395:       continue;\n396:     }\n397:     // Listeners are always added to active_listeners_ list before workers are started.\n398:     // This applies even when the listeners are still waiting for initialization.\n399:     // To avoid confusion in config dump, in that case, we add these listeners to warming\n400:     // listeners config dump rather than active ones.\n401:     DynamicListener* dynamic_listener =\n402:         getOrCreateDynamicListener(listener->name(), *config_dump, listener_map);\n403: \n404:     DynamicListenerState* dump_listener;\n405:     if (workers_started_) {\n406:       dump_listener = dynamic_listener->mutable_active_state();\n407:     } else {\n408:       dump_listener = dynamic_listener->mutable_warming_state();\n409:     }\n410:     fillState(*dump_listener, *listener);\n411:   }\n412: \n413:   for (const auto& listener : warming_listeners_) {\n414:     if (!name_matcher.match(listener->config().name())) {\n415:       continue;\n416:     }\n417:     DynamicListener* dynamic_listener =\n418:         getOrCreateDynamicListener(listener->name(), *config_dump, listener_map);\n419:     DynamicListenerState* dump_listener = dynamic_listener->mutable_warming_state();\n420:     fillState(*dump_listener, *listener);\n421:   }\n422: \n423:   for (const auto& draining_listener : draining_listeners_) {\n424:     if (!name_matcher.match(draining_listener.listener_->config().name())) {\n425:       continue;\n426:     }\n427:     const auto& listener = draining_listener.listener_;\n428:     DynamicListener* dynamic_listener =\n429:         getOrCreateDynamicListener(listener->name(), *config_dump, listener_map);\n430:     DynamicListenerState* dump_listener = dynamic_listener->mutable_draining_state();\n431:     fillState(*dump_listener, *listener);\n432:   }\n433: \n434:   for (const auto& [error_name, error_state] : error_state_tracker_) {\n435:     DynamicListener* dynamic_listener =\n436:         getOrCreateDynamicListener(error_name, *config_dump, listener_map);\n437: \n438:     const envoy::admin::v3::UpdateFailureState& state = *error_state;\n439:     dynamic_listener->mutable_error_state()->CopyFrom(state);\n440:   }\n441: \n442:   // Dump errors not associated with named listeners.\n443:   for (const auto& error : overall_error_state_) {\n444:     config_dump->add_dynamic_listeners()->mutable_error_state()->CopyFrom(*error);\n445:   }\n446: \n447:   return config_dump;\n448: }\n449: \n450: ListenerManagerStats ListenerManagerImpl::generateStats(Stats::Scope& scope) {\n451:   return {ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(POOL_COUNTER(scope), POOL_GAUGE(scope))};\n452: }\n453: \n454: absl::StatusOr<bool>\n455: ListenerManagerImpl::addOrUpdateListener(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n456:                                          const std::string& version_info, bool added_via_api) {\n457:   std::string name;\n458:   if (!config.name().empty()) {\n459:     name = config.name();\n460:   } else {\n461:     // TODO (soulxu): The random uuid name is bad for logging. We can use listening addresses in\n462:     // the log to improve that.\n463:     name = server_.api().randomGenerator().uuid();\n464:   }\n465: \n466:   // TODO(junr03): currently only one ApiListener can be installed via bootstrap to avoid having to\n467:   // build a collection of listeners, and to have to be able to warm and drain the listeners. In the\n468:   // future allow multiple ApiListeners, and allow them to be created via LDS as well as bootstrap.\n469:   if (config.has_api_listener()) {\n470:     if (config.has_internal_listener()) {\n471:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n472:           \"error adding listener named '{}': api_listener and internal_listener cannot be both set\",\n473:           name));\n474:     }\n475:     if (!api_listener_ && !added_via_api) {\n476:       auto listener_or_error = HttpApiListener::create(config, server_, config.name());\n477:       RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_or_error.status());\n478:       api_listener_ = std::move(listener_or_error.value());\n479:       return true;\n480:     } else {\n481:       ENVOY_LOG(warn, \"listener {} can not be added because currently only one ApiListener is \"\n482:                       \"allowed, and it can only be added via bootstrap configuration\");\n483:       return false;\n484:     }\n485:   }\n486: \n487:   // Address field is not required for internal listeners.\n488:   if (!config.has_internal_listener() && !config.has_address()) {\n489:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n490:         fmt::format(\"error adding listener named '{}': address is necessary\", name));\n491:   }\n492: \n493:   auto it = error_state_tracker_.find(name);\n494:   absl::StatusOr<bool> add_or_update_status;\n495:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n496:     add_or_update_status = addOrUpdateListenerInternal(config, version_info, added_via_api, name);\n497:   }\n498:   END_TRY\n499:   CATCH(const EnvoyException& e, { add_or_update_status = absl::InvalidArgumentError(e.what()); })\n500:   if (!add_or_update_status.status().ok()) {\n501:     if (it == error_state_tracker_.end()) {\n502:       it = error_state_tracker_.emplace(name, std::make_unique<UpdateFailureState>()).first;\n503:     }\n504:     TimestampUtil::systemClockToTimestamp(server_.api().timeSource().systemTime(),\n505:                                           *(it->second->mutable_last_update_attempt()));\n506:     it->second->set_details(add_or_update_status.status().message());\n507:     it->second->mutable_failed_configuration()->PackFrom(config);\n508:   }\n509:   return add_or_update_status;\n510: }\n511: \n512: absl::Status\n513: ListenerManagerImpl::setupSocketFactoryForListener(ListenerImpl& new_listener,\n514:                                                    const ListenerImpl& existing_listener) {\n515:   bool same_socket_options = true;\n516:   if (new_listener.reusePort() != existing_listener.reusePort()) {\n517:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n518:         \"Listener {}: reuse port cannot be changed during an update\", new_listener.name()));\n519:   }\n520: \n521:   same_socket_options = existing_listener.socketOptionsEqual(new_listener);\n522:   if (!same_socket_options && new_listener.reusePort() == false) {\n523:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n524:         fmt::format(\"Listener {}: doesn't support update any socket options \"\n525:                     \"when the reuse port isn't enabled\",\n526:                     new_listener.name()));\n527:   }\n528: \n529:   if (!(existing_listener.hasCompatibleAddress(new_listener) && same_socket_options)) {\n530:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(new_listener.name(), new_listener));\n531:   } else {\n532:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(new_listener.cloneSocketFactoryFrom(existing_listener));\n533:   }\n534:   return absl::OkStatus();\n535: }\n536: \n537: absl::StatusOr<bool> ListenerManagerImpl::addOrUpdateListenerInternal(\n538:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config, const std::string& version_info,\n539:     bool added_via_api, const std::string& name) {\n540: \n541:   if (listenersStopped(config)) {\n542:     ENVOY_LOG(\n543:         debug,\n544:         \"listener {} can not be added because listeners in the traffic direction {} are stopped\",\n545:         name, envoy::config::core::v3::TrafficDirection_Name(config.traffic_direction()));\n546:     return false;\n547:   }\n548: \n549:   const uint64_t hash = MessageUtil::hash(config);\n550:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin add/update listener: name={} hash={}\", name, hash);\n551: \n552:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, name);\n553:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, name);\n554: \n555:   // The listener should be updated back to its original state and the warming listener should be\n556:   // removed.\n557:   if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end() &&\n558:       existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end() &&\n559:       (*existing_active_listener)->blockUpdate(hash)) {\n560:     warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n561:     updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n562:     stats_.listener_modified_.inc();\n563:     return true;\n564:   }\n565: \n566:   // Do a quick blocked update check before going further. This check needs to be done against both\n567:   // warming and active.\n568:   if ((existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end() &&\n569:        (*existing_warming_listener)->blockUpdate(hash)) ||\n570:       (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end() &&\n571:        (*existing_active_listener)->blockUpdate(hash))) {\n572:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"duplicate/locked listener '{}'. no add/update\", name);\n573:     return false;\n574:   }\n575: \n576:   ListenerImplPtr new_listener = nullptr;\n577: \n578:   // In place filter chain update depends on the active listener at worker.\n579:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end() &&\n580:       (*existing_active_listener)->supportUpdateFilterChain(config, workers_started_)) {\n581:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"use in place update filter chain update path for listener name={} hash={}\",\n582:               name, hash);\n583:     auto listener_or_error =\n584:         (*existing_active_listener)->newListenerWithFilterChain(config, workers_started_, hash);\n585:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_or_error.status());\n586:     new_listener = std::move(*listener_or_error);\n587:     stats_.listener_in_place_updated_.inc();\n588:   } else {\n589:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"use full listener update path for listener name={} hash={}\", name, hash);\n590:     auto listener_or_error = ListenerImpl::create(config, version_info, *this, name, added_via_api,\n591:                                                   workers_started_, hash);\n592:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_or_error.status());\n593:     new_listener = std::move(*listener_or_error);\n594:   }\n595: \n596:   ListenerImpl& new_listener_ref = *new_listener;\n597: \n598:   bool added = false;\n599:   if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n600:     ASSERT(workers_started_);\n601:     new_listener->debugLog(\"update warming listener\");\n602:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setupSocketFactoryForListener(*new_listener, **existing_warming_listener));\n603:     // In this case we can just replace inline.\n604:     *existing_warming_listener = std::move(new_listener);\n605:   } else if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n606:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setupSocketFactoryForListener(*new_listener, **existing_active_listener));\n607:     // In this case we have no warming listener, so what we do depends on whether workers\n608:     // have been started or not.\n609:     if (workers_started_) {\n610:       new_listener->debugLog(\"add warming listener\");\n611:       warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n612:     } else {\n613:       new_listener->debugLog(\"update active listener\");\n614:       *existing_active_listener = std::move(new_listener);\n615:     }\n616:   } else {\n617:     // We have no warming or active listener so we need to make a new one. What we do depends on\n618:     // whether workers have been started or not.\n619:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(name, *new_listener));\n620:     if (workers_started_) {\n621:       new_listener->debugLog(\"add warming listener\");\n622:       warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n623:     } else {\n624:       new_listener->debugLog(\"add active listener\");\n625:       active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n626:     }\n627: \n628:     added = true;\n629:   }\n630: \n631:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n632:   if (added) {\n633:     stats_.listener_added_.inc();\n634:   } else {\n635:     stats_.listener_modified_.inc();\n636:   }\n637: \n638:   new_listener_ref.initialize();\n639:   return true;\n640: }\n641: \n642: bool ListenerManagerImpl::hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\n643:                                                            const ListenerImpl& listener) {\n644:   for (const auto& existing_listener : list) {\n645:     if (existing_listener->hasDuplicatedAddress(listener)) {\n646:       return true;\n647:     }\n648:   }\n649:   return false;\n650: }\n651: \n652: void ListenerManagerImpl::drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener) {\n653:   // First add the listener to the draining list.\n654:   std::list<DrainingListener>::iterator draining_it = draining_listeners_.emplace(\n655:       draining_listeners_.begin(), std::move(listener), workers_.size());\n656: \n657:   // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\n658:   // restart. Same below inside the lambda.\n659:   stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n660: \n661:   // Tell all workers to stop accepting new connections on this listener.\n662:   draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener\");\n663:   const uint64_t listener_tag = draining_it->listener_->listenerTag();\n664:   stopListener(*draining_it->listener_, {}, [this, listener_tag]() {\n665:     for (auto& listener : draining_listeners_) {\n666:       if (listener.listener_->listenerTag() == listener_tag) {\n667:         maybeCloseSocketsForListener(*listener.listener_);\n668:       }\n669:     }\n670:   });\n671: \n672:   // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\n673:   // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\n674:   draining_it->listener_->localDrainManager().startDrainSequence([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n675:     draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"removing draining listener\");\n676:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n677:       // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\n678:       // to remove the listener.\n679:       worker->removeListener(*draining_it->listener_, [this, draining_it]() -> void {\n680:         // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to the\n681:         // main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the listener\n682:         // while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\n683:         server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n684:           if (--draining_it->workers_pending_removal_ == 0) {\n685:             draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener removal complete\");\n686:             draining_listeners_.erase(draining_it);\n687:             stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n688:           }\n689:         });\n690:       });\n691:     }\n692:   });\n693: \n694:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n695: }\n696: \n697: ListenerManagerImpl::ListenerList::iterator\n698: ListenerManagerImpl::getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name) {\n699:   auto ret = listeners.end();\n700:   for (auto it = listeners.begin(); it != listeners.end(); ++it) {\n701:     if ((*it)->name() == name) {\n702:       // There should only ever be a single listener per name in the list. We could return faster\n703:       // but take the opportunity to assert that fact.\n704:       ASSERT(ret == listeners.end());\n705:       ret = it;\n706:     }\n707:   }\n708:   return ret;\n709: }\n710: \n711: std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n712: ListenerManagerImpl::listeners(ListenerState state) {\n713:   std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>> ret;\n714: \n715:   size_t size = 0;\n716:   size += state & WARMING ? warming_listeners_.size() : 0;\n717:   size += state & ACTIVE ? active_listeners_.size() : 0;\n718:   size += state & DRAINING ? draining_listeners_.size() : 0;\n719:   ret.reserve(size);\n720: \n721:   if (state & WARMING) {\n722:     for (const auto& listener : warming_listeners_) {\n723:       ret.push_back(*listener);\n724:     }\n725:   }\n726:   if (state & ACTIVE) {\n727:     for (const auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n728:       ret.push_back(*listener);\n729:     }\n730:   }\n731:   if (state & DRAINING) {\n732:     for (const auto& draining_listener : draining_listeners_) {\n733:       ret.push_back(*(draining_listener.listener_));\n734:     }\n735:   }\n736:   return ret;\n737: }\n738: \n739: bool ListenerManagerImpl::doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n740:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n741:     for (auto& socket_factory : listener.listenSocketFactories()) {\n742:       absl::Status success = (socket_factory->doFinalPreWorkerInit());\n743:       if (!success.ok()) {\n744:         ENVOY_LOG(error, \"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\",\n745:                   listener.name(), success.message());\n746:         return false;\n747:       }\n748:     }\n749:     return true;\n750:   }\n751:   END_TRY\n752:   CATCH(EnvoyException & e, {\n753:     ENVOY_LOG(error, \"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\", listener.name(),\n754:               e.what());\n755:     return false;\n756:   });\n757: }\n758: \n759: void ListenerManagerImpl::addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker,\n760:                                               absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\n761:                                               ListenerImpl& listener,\n762:                                               ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback) {\n763:   if (overridden_listener.has_value()) {\n764:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"replacing existing listener {}\", overridden_listener.value());\n765:   }\n766:   worker.addListener(\n767:       overridden_listener, listener,\n768:       [this, completion_callback]() -> void {\n769:         // The add listener completion runs on the worker thread. Post back to the main thread to\n770:         // avoid locking.\n771:         server_.dispatcher().post([this, completion_callback]() -> void {\n772:           stats_.listener_create_success_.inc();\n773:           if (completion_callback) {\n774:             completion_callback();\n775:           }\n776:         });\n777:       },\n778:       server_.runtime(), server_.api().randomGenerator());\n779: }\n780: \n781: void ListenerManagerImpl::onListenerWarmed(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n782:   // The warmed listener should be added first so that the worker will accept new connections\n783:   // when it stops listening on the old listener.\n784:   if (!doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(listener)) {\n785:     incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n786:     // TODO(mattklein123): Technically we don't need to remove the active listener if one exists.\n787:     // The following call will remove both.\n788:     removeListenerInternal(listener.name(), true);\n789:     return;\n790:   }\n791:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n792:     addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, listener, nullptr);\n793:   }\n794: \n795:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n796:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n797: \n798:   (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"warm complete. updating active listener\");\n799:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n800:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n801:     // state.\n802:     auto listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n803:     *existing_active_listener = std::move(*existing_warming_listener);\n804:     drainListener(std::move(listener));\n805:   } else {\n806:     active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(*existing_warming_listener));\n807:   }\n808: \n809:   warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n810:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n811: }\n812: \n813: void ListenerManagerImpl::inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n814:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n815:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n816:   ASSERT(existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end());\n817:   ASSERT(*existing_warming_listener != nullptr);\n818: \n819:   (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"execute in place filter chain update\");\n820: \n821:   // Now that in place filter chain update was decided, the replaced listener must be in active\n822:   // list. It requires stop/remove listener procedure cancelling the in placed update if any.\n823:   ASSERT(existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end());\n824:   ASSERT(*existing_active_listener != nullptr);\n825: \n826:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n827:     // Explicitly override the existing listener with a new listener config.\n828:     addListenerToWorker(*worker, listener.listenerTag(), listener, nullptr);\n829:   }\n830: \n831:   auto previous_listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n832:   *existing_active_listener = std::move(*existing_warming_listener);\n833:   // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainFilterChains` as it depends on\n834:   // their state.\n835:   drainFilterChains(std::move(previous_listener), **existing_active_listener);\n836: \n837:   warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n838:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n839: }\n840: \n841: void ListenerManagerImpl::drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\n842:                                             ListenerImpl& new_listener) {\n843:   // First add the listener to the draining list.\n844:   std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager>::iterator draining_group =\n845:       draining_filter_chains_manager_.emplace(draining_filter_chains_manager_.begin(),\n846:                                               std::move(draining_listener), workers_.size());\n847:   draining_group->getDrainingListener().diffFilterChain(\n848:       new_listener, [&draining_group](Network::DrainableFilterChain& filter_chain) mutable {\n849:         filter_chain.startDraining();\n850:         draining_group->addFilterChainToDrain(filter_chain);\n851:       });\n852:   auto filter_chain_size = draining_group->numDrainingFilterChains();\n853:   stats_.total_filter_chains_draining_.add(filter_chain_size);\n854:   draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n855:       absl::StrCat(\"draining \", filter_chain_size, \" filter chains in listener \",\n856:                    draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\n857: \n858:   // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\n859:   // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\n860:   draining_group->startDrainSequence(\n861:       server_.options().drainTime(), server_.dispatcher(), [this, draining_group]() -> void {\n862:         draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n863:             absl::StrCat(\"removing draining filter chains from listener \",\n864:                          draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\n865:         for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n866:           // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\n867:           // to remove the filter chains.\n868:           worker->removeFilterChains(\n869:               draining_group->getDrainingListenerTag(), draining_group->getDrainingFilterChains(),\n870:               [this, draining_group]() -> void {\n871:                 // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to\n872:                 // the main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the\n873:                 // listener while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\n874:                 server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_group]() -> void {\n875:                   if (draining_group->decWorkersPendingRemoval() == 0) {\n876:                     draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n877:                         absl::StrCat(\"draining filter chains from listener \",\n878:                                      draining_group->getDrainingListener().name(), \" complete\"));\n879:                     stats_.total_filter_chains_draining_.sub(\n880:                         draining_group->numDrainingFilterChains());\n881:                     draining_filter_chains_manager_.erase(draining_group);\n882:                   }\n883:                 });\n884:               });\n885:         }\n886:       });\n887:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n888: }\n889: \n890: uint64_t ListenerManagerImpl::numConnections() const {\n891:   uint64_t num_connections = 0;\n892:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n893:     num_connections += worker->numConnections();\n894:   }\n895: \n896:   return num_connections;\n897: }\n898: \n899: bool ListenerManagerImpl::removeListener(const std::string& name) {\n900:   return removeListenerInternal(name, true);\n901: }\n902: \n903: bool ListenerManagerImpl::removeListenerInternal(const std::string& name,\n904:                                                  bool dynamic_listeners_only) {\n905:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin remove listener: name={}\", name);\n906: \n907:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, name);\n908:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, name);\n909:   if ((existing_warming_listener == warming_listeners_.end() ||\n910:        (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_warming_listener)->blockRemove())) &&\n911:       (existing_active_listener == active_listeners_.end() ||\n912:        (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_active_listener)->blockRemove()))) {\n913:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"unknown/locked listener '{}'. no remove\", name);\n914:     return false;\n915:   }\n916: \n917:   // Destroy a warming listener directly.\n918:   if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n919:     (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"removing warming listener\");\n920:     warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n921:   }\n922: \n923:   // If there is an active listener it needs to be moved to draining after workers have started, or\n924:   // destroyed directly.\n925:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n926:     // Listeners in active_listeners_ are added to workers after workers start, so we drain\n927:     // listeners only after this occurs.\n928:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n929:     // state.\n930:     auto listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n931:     active_listeners_.erase(existing_active_listener);\n932:     if (workers_started_) {\n933:       drainListener(std::move(listener));\n934:     }\n935:   }\n936: \n937:   stats_.listener_removed_.inc();\n938:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n939:   return true;\n940: }\n941: \n942: absl::Status ListenerManagerImpl::startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog,\n943:                                                std::function<void()> callback) {\n944:   ENVOY_LOG(info, \"all dependencies initialized. starting workers\");\n945:   ASSERT(!workers_started_);\n946:   workers_started_ = true;\n947:   uint32_t i = 0;\n948: \n949:   absl::BlockingCounter workers_waiting_to_run(workers_.size());\n950:   std::function<void()> worker_started_running = [&workers_waiting_to_run]() {\n951:     workers_waiting_to_run.DecrementCount();\n952:   };\n953: \n954:   // We can not use \"Cleanup\" to simplify this logic here, because it results in a issue if Envoy is\n955:   // killed before workers are actually started. Specifically the AdminRequestGetStatsAndKill test\n956:   // case in main_common_test fails with ASAN error if we use \"Cleanup\" here.\n957:   const auto listeners_pending_init =\n958:       std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size() * active_listeners_.size());\n959:   ASSERT(warming_listeners_.empty());\n960:   // We need to protect against inline deletion so have to use iterators directly.\n961:   for (auto listener_it = active_listeners_.begin(); listener_it != active_listeners_.end();) {\n962:     auto& listener = *listener_it;\n963:     listener_it++;\n964: \n965:     absl::StatusOr<bool> init_status = doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(*listener);\n966:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(init_status.status());\n967: \n968:     if (!*init_status) {\n969:       incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n970:       removeListenerInternal(listener->name(), false);\n971:       continue;\n972:     }\n973:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n974:       addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, *listener,\n975:                           [this, listeners_pending_init, callback]() {\n976:                             if (--(*listeners_pending_init) == 0) {\n977:                               stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n978:                               callback();\n979:                             }\n980:                           });\n981:     }\n982:   }\n983:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n984:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"starting worker {}\", i);\n985:     worker->start(guard_dog, worker_started_running);\n986:     if (enable_dispatcher_stats_) {\n987:       worker->initializeStats(*scope_);\n988:     }\n989:     i++;\n990:   }\n991: \n992:   // Wait for workers to start running.\n993:   workers_waiting_to_run.Wait();\n994: \n995:   if (active_listeners_.empty()) {\n996:     stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n997:     callback();\n998:   }\n999:   return absl::OkStatus();\n1000: }\n1001: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.33.0@75cca99ee9fea5a6b699af6a2124ec7404fe5d13/-/blob/source/common/listener_manager/listener_manager_impl.cc%3FL1-1000"
  }
}

#define ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(COUNTER, GAUGE)                                                 \\\\\\n144:   COUNTER(listener_added)                                                                          \\\\\\n145:   COUNTER(listener_create_failure)                                                                 \\\\\\n146:   COUNTER(listener_create_success)                                                                 \\\\\\n147:   COUNTER(listener_in_place_updated)                                                               \\\\\\n148:   COUNTER(listener_modified)                                                                       \\\\\\n149:   COUNTER(listener_removed)                                                                        \\\\\\n150:                             \\\\\\n154:   GAUGE(total_listeners_warming, NeverImport)                                                      \\\\\\n155:   GAUGE(workers_started, NeverImport)\\n156: \\n157: /**\\n158:  * Struct definition for all listener manager stats. @see stats_macros.h\\n159:  */\\n160: struct ListenerManagerStats {\\n161:   ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT, GENERATE_GAUGE_STRUCT)\\n162: };\\n163: \\n164: /**\\n165:  * Provides the draining filter chains and the functionality to schedule listener destroy.\\n166:  */\\n167: class DrainingFilterChainsManager {\\n168: public:\\n169:   DrainingFilterChainsManager(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\\n170:                               uint64_t workers_pending_removal);\\n171:   uint64_t getDrainingListenerTag() const { return draining_listener_->listenerTag(); }\\n172:   const std::list<const Network::FilterChain*>& getDrainingFilterChains() const {\\n173:     return draining_filter_chains_;\\n174:   }\\n175:   ListenerImpl& getDrainingListener() const { return *draining_listener_; }\\n176:   uint64_t decWorkersPendingRemoval() { return --workers_pending_removal_; }\\n177: \\n178:   // Schedule listener destroy.\\n179:   void startDrainSequence(std::chrono::seconds drain_time, Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher,\\n180:                           std::function<void()> completion) {\\n181:     drain_sequence_completion_ = completion;\\n182:     ASSERT(!drain_timer_);\\n183: \\n184:     drain_timer_ = dispatcher.createTimer([this]() -> void { drain_sequence_completion_(); });\\n185:     drain_timer_->enableTimer(drain_time);\\n186:   }\\n187: \\n188:   void addFilterChainToDrain(const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain) {\\n189:     draining_filter_chains_.push_back(&filter_chain);\\n190:   }\\n191: \\n192:   uint32_t numDrainingFilterChains() const { return draining_filter_chains_.size(); }\\n193: \\n194: private:\\n195:   ListenerImplPtr draining_listener_;\\n196:   std::list<const Network::FilterChain*> draining_filter_chains_;\\n197: \\n198:   uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\\n199:   Event::TimerPtr drain_timer_;\\n200:   std::function<void()> drain_sequence_completion_;\\n201: };\\n202: \\n203: /**\\n204:  * Implementation of ListenerManager.\\n205:  */\\n206: class ListenerManagerImpl : public nullptr);\\n222:     lds_api_ = factory_->createLdsApi(lds_config, lds_resources_locator);\\n223:   }\\n224:   std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\\n225: Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options) override;\\n231:   void stopWorkers() override;\\n232:   void beginListenerUpdate() override { error_state_tracker_.clear(); }\\n233:   void endListenerUpdate(FailureStates&& failure_state) override;\\n234:   bool isWorkerStarted() override { return workers_started_; }\\n235:   Http::Context& httpContext() { return server_.httpContext(); }\\n236:   ApiListenerOptRef apiListener() override;\\n237: \\n238:   Quic::QuicStatNames& quicStatNames() { return quic_stat_names_; }\\n239: \\n240:   Instance& server_;\\n241: initialization is completed on worker.\\n247:    */\\n248:   using ListenerCompletionCallback = std::function<void()>;\\n249: \\n250:   absl::StatusOr<bool>\\n251:   addOrUpdateListenerInternal(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\\n252:                               const std::string& version_info, bool added_via_api,\\n253:                               const std::string& name);\\n254:   bool removeListenerInternal(const std::string& listener_name, bool dynamic_listeners_only);\\n255: \\n256:   struct DrainingListener {\\n257:     DrainingListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener, uint64_t workers_pending_removal)\\n258:         : listener_(std::move(listener)), workers_pending_removal_(workers_pending_removal) {}\\n259: \\n260:     ListenerImplPtr listener_;\\n261:     uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\\n262:   };\\n263: \\n264:   bool doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener);\\n265:   void addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker, absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\\n266:                            ListenerImpl& listener, ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback);\\n267: \\n268:   ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr dumpListenerConfigs(const Matchers::StringMatcher& name_matcher);\\n269:   static ListenerManagerStats generateStats(Stats::Scope& scope);\\n270:   static bool hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\\n271:                                                const ListenerImpl& listener);\\n272:   void updateWarmingActiveGauges() {\\n273:     // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\\n274:     // restart.\\n275:     stats_.total_listeners_warming_.set(warming_listeners_.size());\\n276:     stats_.total_listeners_active_.set(active_listeners_.size());\\n277:   }\\n278:   bool listenersStopped(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\\n279:     // Currently all listeners in a given direction are stopped because of the way admin\\n280:     // drain_listener functionality is implemented. This needs to be revisited, if that changes - if\\n281:     // we support drain by listener name,for example.\\n282:     return stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::All ||\\n283:            (stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::InboundOnly &&\\n284:             config.traffic_direction() == envoy::config::core::v3::INBOUND);\\n285:   }\\n286:   void incListenerCreateFailureStat() {\\n287:     // TODO(mattklein123): Now that we no longer create sockets and apply options on workers,\\n288:     // this stat is confusingly named and should be more specific, however it's unclear if it's\\n289:     // worth changing.\\n290:     stats_.listener_create_failure_.inc();\\n291:   }\\n292: \\n293:   /**\\n294:    * Mark a listener for draining. The listener will no longer be considered active but will remain\\n295:    * present to allow connection draining.\\n296:    * @param listener supplies the listener to drain.\\n297:    */\\n298:   void drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener);\\n299: \\n300:   /**\\n301:    * Start to draining filter chains that are owned by draining listener but not owned by\\n302:    * new_listener. The new listener should have taken over the listener socket and partial of the\\n303:    * filter chains from listener. This method is used by in place filter chain update.\\n304:    * @param draining_listener supplies the listener to be replaced.\\n305:    * @param new_listener supplies the new listener config which is going to replace the draining\\n306:    * listener.\\n307:    */\\n308:   void drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener, ListenerImpl& new_listener);\\n309: \\n310:   /**\\n311:    * Stop a listener. The listener will stop accepting new connections and its socket will be\\n312:    * closed.\\n313:    * @param listener supplies the listener to stop.\\n314:    * @param options additional options to be passed through to shutdownListener.\\n315:    * @param completion supplies the completion to be called when all workers are stopped accepting\\n316:    * new connections. This completion is called on the main thread.\\n317:    */\\n318:   void stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\\n319:                     const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\\n320:                     std::function<void()> completion);\\n321: \\n322:   /**\\n323:    * Get a listener by name. This routine is used because listeners have inherent order in static\\n324:    * configuration and especially for tests. Thus, we can't use a map.\\n325:    * @param listeners supplies the listener list to look in.\\n326:    * @param name supplies the name to search for.\\n327:    */\\n328:   ListenerList::iterator getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name);\\n329: \\n330:   absl::Status setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(const std::string& name, ListenerImpl& listener);\\n331:   absl::Status createListenSocketFactory(ListenerImpl& listener);\\n332: \\n333:   void maybeCloseSocketsForListener(ListenerImpl& listener);\\n334:   absl::Status setupSocketFactoryForListener(ListenerImpl& new_listener,\\n335:                                              const ListenerImpl& existing_listener);\\n336: \\n337:   ApiListenerPtr api_listener_;\\n338:   // Active listeners are listeners that are currently accepting new connections on the workers.\\n339:   ListenerList active_listeners_;\\n340:   // Warming listeners are listeners that may need further initialization via the listener's init\\n341:   // manager. For example, RDS, or in the future KDS. Once a listener is done warming it will\\n342:   // be transitioned to active.\\n343:   ListenerList warming_listeners_;\\n344:   // Draining listeners are listeners that are in the process of being drained and removed. They\\n345:   // go through two phases where first the workers stop accepting new connections and existing\\n346:   // connections are drained. Then after that time period the listener is removed from all workers\\n347:   // and any remaining connections are closed.\\n348:   std::list<DrainingListener> draining_listeners_;\\n349:   std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager> draining_filter_chains_manager_;\\n350: \\n351:   std::vector<WorkerPtr> workers_;\\n352:   bool workers_started_{};\\n353:   absl::optional<StopListenersType> stop_listeners_type_;\\n354:   Stats::ScopeSharedPtr scope_;\\n355:   ListenerManagerStats stats_;\\n356:   ConfigTracker::EntryOwnerPtr config_tracker_entry_;\\n357:   LdsApiPtr lds_api_;\\n358: "{\"content\":\"58: \\n59: class ListenerManagerImplTest : public testing::TestWithParam<bool> {\\n60: public:\\n61:   // reuse_port is the default on Linux for TCP. On other platforms even if set it is disabled\\n62:   // and the user is warned. For UDP it's always the default even if not effective.\\n63:   static constexpr ListenerComponentFactory::BindType default_bind_type =\\n64: #ifdef __linux__\\n65:       ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort;\\n66: #else\\n67:       ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoReusePort;\\n68: #endif\\n69: \\n70: protected:\\n71:   ListenerManagerImplTest()\\n72:       : listener_factory_ptr_(std::make_unique<NiceMock<MockListenerComponentFactory>>()),\\n73:         listener_factory_(*listener_factory_ptr_),\\n74:         api_(Api::createApiForTest(server_.api_.random_)), use_matcher_(GetParam()) {}\\n75: \\n76:   void SetUp() override {\\n77:     ON_CALL(server_, api()).WillByDefault(ReturnRef(*api_));\\n78:     ON_CALL(*server_.server_factory_context_, api()).WillByDefault(ReturnRef(*api_));\\n79:     EXPECT_CALL(worker_factory_, createWorker_()).WillOnce(Return(worker_));\\n80:     ON_CALL(server_.validation_context_, staticValidationVisitor())\\n81:         .WillByDefault(ReturnRef(validation_visitor));\\n82:     ON_CALL(server_.validation_context_, dynamicValidationVisitor())\\n83:         .WillByDefault(ReturnRef(validation_visitor));\\n84:     manager_ = std::make_unique<ListenerManagerImpl>(server_, std::move(listener_factory_ptr_),\\n85:                                                      worker_factory_, enable_dispatcher_stats_,\\n86:                                                      server_.quic_stat_names_);\\n87: \\n88:     // Use real filter loading by default.\\n89:     ON_CALL(listener_factory_, createNetworkFilterFactoryList(_, _))\\n90:         .WillByDefault(Invoke(\\n91: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.33.0@75cca99ee9fea5a6b699af6a2124ec7404fe5d13/-/blob/test/common/listener_manager/listener_manager_impl_test.h%3FL58-90\"}"

draining_filter_chains_;\\n174:   }\\n175:   ListenerImpl& getDrainingListener() const { return *draining_listener_; }\\n176:   uint64_t decWorkersPendingRemoval() { return --workers_pending_removal_; completion) {\\n181:     drain_sequence_completion_ = completion;\\n182:     ASSERT(!drain_timer_);\\n183: \\n184:     drain_timer_ = dispatcher.createTimer([this]() -> void { drain_sequence_completion_(); });\\n185:     drain_timer_->enableTimer(drain_time);\\n186:   }\\n187: \\n188:   void addFilterChainToDrain(const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain) {\\n189: drain_timer_;\\n200:   std::function<void()> drain_sequence_completion_;\\n201: };\\n202: \\n203: /**\\n204:  * Implementation of ListenerManager.\\n205:  */\\n206: class ListenerManagerImpl : public nullptr);\\n222:     lds_api_ = factory_->createLdsApi(lds_config, lds_resources_locator);\\n223:   }\\n224:   std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\\n225: endListenerUpdate(FailureStates&& failure_state) override;\\n234:   bool isWorkerStarted() override { return workers_started_; }\\n235:   Http::Context& httpContext() { return server_.httpContext(); }\\n236:   ApiListenerOptRef apiListener() override;\\n237: \\n238:   Quic::QuicStatNames& quicStatNames() { return quic_stat_names_; }\\n239: \\n240:   Instance& server_;\\n241: initialization is completed on worker.\\n247:    */\\n248:   using ListenerCompletionCallback = std::function<void()>;\\n249: \\n250:   absl::StatusOr<bool>\\n251:   addOrUpdateListenerInternal(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\\n252:                               const std::string& version_info, bool added_via_api,\\n253:                               const std::string& name);\\n254:   bool removeListenerInternal(const std::string& listener_name, bool dynamic_listeners_only);\\n255: \\n256:   struct DrainingListener {\\n257:     DrainingListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener, uint64_t workers_pending_removal)\\n258:         : listener_(std::move(listener)), workers_pending_removal_(workers_pending_removal) {}\\n259: \\n260:     ListenerImplPtr listener_;\\n261:     uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\\n262:   };\\n263: \\n264:   bool doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener);\\n265:   void addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker, absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\\n266:                            ListenerImpl& listener, ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback);\\n267: \\n268:   ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr dumpListenerConfigs(const Matchers::StringMatcher& name_matcher);\\n269:   static ListenerManagerStats generateStats(Stats::Scope& scope);\\n270:   static bool hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\\n271:                                                const ListenerImpl& listener);\\n272:   void updateWarmingActiveGauges() {\\n273:     // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\\n274:     // restart.\\n275:     stats_.total_listeners_warming_.set(warming_listeners_.size());\\n276:     stats_.total_listeners_active_.set(active_listeners_.size());\\n277:   }\\n278:   bool listenersStopped(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\\n279:     // Currently all listeners in a given direction are stopped because of the way admin\\n280:     // drain_listener functionality is implemented. This needs to be revisited, if that changes - if\\n281:     // we support drain by listener name,for example.\\n282:     return stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::All ||\\n283:            (stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::InboundOnly &&\\n284:             config.traffic_direction() == envoy::config::core::v3::INBOUND);\\n285:   }\\n286:   void incListenerCreateFailureStat() {\\n287:     // TODO(mattklein123): Now that we no longer create sockets and apply options on workers,\\n288:     // this stat is confusingly named and should be more specific, however it's unclear if it's\\n289:     // worth changing.\\n290:     stats_.listener_create_failure_.inc();\\n291:   }\\n292: \\n293:   /**\\n294:    * Mark a listener for draining. The listener will no longer be considered active but will remain\\n295:    * present to allow connection draining.\\n296:    * @param listener supplies the listener to drain.\\n297:    */\\n298:   void drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener);\\n299: \\n300:   /**\\n301:    * Start to draining filter chains that are owned by draining listener but not owned by\\n302:    * new_listener. The new listener should have taken over the listener socket and partial of the\\n303:    * filter chains from listener. This method is used by in place filter chain update.\\n304:    * @param draining_listener supplies the listener to be replaced.\\n305:    * @param new_listener supplies the new listener config which is going to replace the draining\\n306:    * listener.\\n307:    */\\n308:   void drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener, ListenerImpl& new_listener);\\n309: \\n310:   /**\\n311:    * Stop a listener. The listener will stop accepting new connections and its socket will be\\n312:    * closed.\\n313:    * @param listener supplies the listener to stop.\\n314:    * @param options additional options to be passed through to shutdownListener.\\n315:    * @param completion supplies the completion to be called when all workers are stopped accepting\\n316:    * new connections. This completion is called on the main thread.\\n317:    */\\n318:   void stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\\n319:                     const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\\n320:                     std::function<void()> completion);\\n321: \\n322:   /**\\n323:    * Get a listener by name. This routine is used because listeners have inherent order in static\\n324:    * configuration and especially for tests. Thus, we can't use a map.\\n325:    * @param listeners supplies the listener list to look in.\\n326:    * @param name supplies the name to search for.\\n327:    */\\n328:   ListenerList::iterator getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name);\\n329: \\n330:   absl::Status setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(const std::string& name, ListenerImpl& listener);\\n331:   absl::Status createListenSocketFactory(ListenerImpl& listener);\\n332: \\n333:   void maybeCloseSocketsForListener(ListenerImpl& listener);\\n334:   absl::Status setupSocketFactoryForListener(ListenerImpl& new_listener,\\n335:                                              const ListenerImpl& existing_listener);\\n336: \\n337:   ApiListenerPtr api_listener_;\\n338:   // Active listeners are listeners that are currently accepting new connections on the workers.\\n339:   ListenerList active_listeners_;\\n340:   // Warming listeners are listeners that may need further initialization via the listener's init\\n341:   // manager. For example, RDS, or in the future KDS. Once a listener is done warming it will\\n342:   // be transitioned to active.\\n343:   ListenerList warming_listeners_;\\n344:   // Draining listeners are listeners that are in the process of being drained and removed. They\\n345:   // go through two phases where first the workers stop accepting new connections and existing\\n346:   // connections are drained. Then after that time period the listener is removed from all workers\\n347:   // and any remaining connections are closed.\\n348:   std::list<DrainingListener> draining_listeners_;\\n349:   std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager> draining_filter_chains_manager_;\\n350: \\n351:   std::vector<WorkerPtr> workers_;\\n352:   bool workers_started_{};\\n353:   absl::optional<StopListenersType> stop_listeners_type_;\\n354:   Stats::ScopeSharedPtr scope_;\\n355:   ListenerManagerStats stats_;\\n356:   ConfigTracker::EntryOwnerPtr config_tracker_entry_;\\n357:   LdsApiPtr lds_api_;\\n358:   const bool enable_dispatcher_stats_{};\\n359:   using UpdateFailureState = envoy::admin::v3::UpdateFailureState;\\n360:   absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, std::unique_ptr<UpdateFailureState>> error_state_tracker_;\\n361:   FailureStates overall_error_state_;\\n362:   Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names_;\\n363: };\\n364: \\n365: class ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder : public FilterChainFactoryBuilder {\\n366: public:\\n367:   ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder(\\n368:       ListenerImpl& listener, Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context);\\n369: \\n370:   absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\\n371:   buildFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\\n372:   "content": "#pragma once\n\n#include <memory>\n\n#include \"envoy/admin/v3/config_dump.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/address.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/listener/v3/listener.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/config/listener/v3/listener_components.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/network/filter.h\"\n#include \"envoy/network/listen_socket.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/api_listener.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/filter_config.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/instance.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/listener_manager.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/transport_socket_config.h\"\n#include \"envoy/server/worker.h\"\n#include \"envoy/stats/scope.h\"\n\n#include \"source/common/config/well_known_names.h\"\n#include \"source/common/filter/config_discovery_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_factory_context_callback.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/lds_api.h\"\n#include \"source/common/listener_manager/listener_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/quic/quic_stat_names.h\"\n#include \"source/server/listener_manager_factory.h\"\n\nnamespace Envoy {\nnamespace Server {\n\nnamespace Configuration {\nclass TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl;\n}\n\nclass ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder;\n\n/**\n * Prod implementation of ListenerComponentFactory that creates real sockets and attempts to fetch\n * sockets from the parent process via the hot restarter. The filter factory list is created from\n * statically registered filters.\n */\nclass ProdListenerComponentFactory : public ListenerComponentFactory,\n                                     Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\npublic:\n  ProdListenerComponentFactory(Instance& server) : server_(server) {}\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createNetworkFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static absl::StatusOr<Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList> createNetworkFilterFactoryListImpl(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::Filter>& filters,\n      Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContext& filter_chain_factory_context,\n      Filter::NetworkFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\n\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static absl::StatusOr<Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList> createListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context,\n      Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\n\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createUdpListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static absl::StatusOr<std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb>>\n  createUdpListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context);\n\n  /**\n   * Static worker for createQuicListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\n   */\n  static absl::StatusOr<Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList>\n  createQuicListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context,\n      Filter::QuicListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\n\n  static Network::ListenerFilterMatcherSharedPtr\n  createListenerFilterMatcher(const envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter& listener_filter);\n\n  // Server::ListenerComponentFactory\n  LdsApiPtr createLdsApi(const envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource& lds_config,\n                         const xds::core::v3::ResourceLocator* lds_resources_locator) override {\n    return std::make_unique<LdsApiImpl>(\n        lds_config, lds_resources_locator, server_.clusterManager(), server_.initManager(),\n        *server_.stats().rootScope(), server_.listenerManager(),\n        server_.messageValidationContext().dynamicValidationVisitor());\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList> createNetworkFilterFactoryList(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::Filter>& filters,\n      Server::Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContext& filter_chain_factory_context) override {\n    return createNetworkFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, filter_chain_factory_context,\n                                              network_config_provider_manager_);\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList> createListenerFilterFactoryList(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\n    return createListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context,\n                                               tcp_listener_config_provider_manager_);\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb>>\n  createUdpListenerFilterFactoryList(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\n    return createUdpListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context);\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList> createQuicListenerFilterFactoryList(\n      const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n      Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\n    return createQuicListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context,\n                                                   quic_listener_config_provider_manager_);\n  }\n  absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> createListenSocket(\n      Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address, Network::Socket::Type socket_type,\n      const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options, BindType bind_type,\n      const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options, uint32_t worker_index) override;\n\n  DrainManagerPtr\n  createDrainManager(envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener::DrainType drain_type) override;\n  uint64_t nextListenerTag() override { return next_listener_tag_++; }\n  Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl*\n  getTcpListenerConfigProviderManager() override {\n    return &tcp_listener_config_provider_manager_;\n  }\n\nprivate:\n  Instance& server_;\n  uint64_t next_listener_tag_{1};\n  Filter::NetworkFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl network_config_provider_manager_;\n  Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl tcp_listener_config_provider_manager_;\n  Filter::QuicListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl quic_listener_config_provider_manager_;\n};\n\nclass ListenerImpl;\nusing ListenerImplPtr = std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>;\n\n/**\n * All listener manager stats. @see stats_macros.h\n */\n#define ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(COUNTER, GAUGE)                                                 \\\n  COUNTER(listener_added)                                                                          \\\n  COUNTER(listener_create_failure)                                                                 \\\n  COUNTER(listener_create_success)                                                                 \\\n  COUNTER(listener_in_place_updated)                                                               \\\n  COUNTER(listener_modified)                                                                       \\\n  COUNTER(listener_removed)                                                                        \\\n  COUNTER(listener_stopped)                                                                        \\\n  GAUGE(total_filter_chains_draining, NeverImport)                                                 \\\n  GAUGE(total_listeners_active, NeverImport)                                                       \\\n  GAUGE(total_listeners_draining, NeverImport)                                                     \\\n  GAUGE(total_listeners_warming, NeverImport)                                                      \\\n  GAUGE(workers_started, NeverImport)\n\n/**\n * Struct definition for all listener manager stats. @see stats_macros.h\n */\nstruct ListenerManagerStats {\n  ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT, GENERATE_GAUGE_STRUCT)\n};\n\n/**\n * Provides the draining filter chains and the functionality to schedule listener destroy.\n */\nclass DrainingFilterChainsManager {\npublic:\n  DrainingFilterChainsManager(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\n                              uint64_t workers_pending_removal);\n  uint64_t getDrainingListenerTag() const { return draining_listener_->listenerTag(); }\n  const std::list<const Network::FilterChain*>& getDrainingFilterChains() const {\n    return draining_filter_chains_;\n  }\n  ListenerImpl& getDrainingListener() const { return *draining_listener_; }\n  uint64_t decWorkersPendingRemoval() { return --workers_pending_removal_; }\n\n  // Schedule listener destroy.\n  void startDrainSequence(std::chrono::seconds drain_time, Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher,\n                          std::function<void()> completion) {\n    drain_sequence_completion_ = completion;\n    ASSERT(!drain_timer_);\n\n    drain_timer_ = dispatcher.createTimer([this]() -> void { drain_sequence_completion_(); });\n    drain_timer_->enableTimer(drain_time);\n  }\n\n  void addFilterChainToDrain(const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain) {\n    draining_filter_chains_.push_back(&filter_chain);\n  }\n\n  uint32_t numDrainingFilterChains() const { return draining_filter_chains_.size(); }\n\nprivate:\n  ListenerImplPtr draining_listener_;\n  std::list<const Network::FilterChain*> draining_filter_chains_;\n\n  uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\n  Event::TimerPtr drain_timer_;\n  std::function<void()> drain_sequence_completion_;\n};\n\n/**\n * Implementation of ListenerManager.\n */\nclass ListenerOrchestrator : public ListenerManager, Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\npublic:\n  ListenerOrchestrator(Instance& server, std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory>&& factory,\n                       WorkerFactory& worker_factory, bool enable_dispatcher_stats,\n                       Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names);\n\n  void onListenerWarmed(ListenerImpl& listener);\n  void inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener);\n\n  // Server::ListenerManager\n  absl::StatusOr<bool> addOrUpdateListener(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n                                           const std::string& version_info,\n                                           bool added_via_api) override;\n  void createLdsApi(const envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource& lds_config,\n                    const xds::core::v3::ResourceLocator* lds_resources_locator) override {\n    ASSERT(lds_api_ == nullptr);\n    lds_api_ = factory_->createLdsApi(lds_config, lds_resources_locator);\n  }\n  std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n  listeners(ListenerState state = ListenerState::ACTIVE) override;\n  uint64_t numConnections() const override;\n  bool removeListener(const std::string& listener_name) override;\n  absl::Status startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog, std::function<void()> callback) override;\n  void stopListeners(StopListenersType stop_listeners_type,\n                     const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options) override;\n  void stopWorkers() override;\n  void beginListenerUpdate() override { error_state_tracker_.clear(); }\n  void endListenerUpdate(FailureStates&& failure_state) override;\n  bool isWorkerStarted() override { return workers_started_; }\n  Http::Context& httpContext() { return server_.httpContext(); }\n  ApiListenerOptRef apiListener() override;\n\n  Quic::QuicStatNames& quicStatNames() { return quic_stat_names_; }\n\n  Instance& server_;\n  std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory> factory_;\n\nprivate:\n  using ListenerList = std::list<ListenerImplPtr>;\n  /**\n   * Callback invoked when a listener initialization is completed on worker.\n   */\n  using ListenerCompletionCallback = std::function<void()>;\n\n  absl::StatusOr<bool>\n  addOrUpdateListenerInternal(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n                              const std::string& version_info, bool added_via_api,\n                              const std::string& name);\n  bool removeListenerInternal(const std::string& listener_name, bool dynamic_listeners_only);\n\n  struct DrainingListener {\n    DrainingListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener, uint64_t workers_pending_removal)\n        : listener_(std::move(listener)), workers_pending_removal_(workers_pending_removal) {}\n\n    ListenerImplPtr listener_;\n    uint64_t workers_pending_removal_;\n  };\n\n  bool doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener);\n  void addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker, absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\n                           ListenerImpl& listener, ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback);\n\n  ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr dumpListenerConfigs(const Matchers::StringMatcher& name_matcher);\n  static ListenerManagerStats generateStats(Stats::Scope& scope);\n  static bool hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\n                                               const ListenerImpl& listener);\n  void updateWarmingActiveGauges() {\n    // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\n    // restart.\n    stats_.total_listeners_warming_.set(warming_listeners_.size());\n    stats_.total_listeners_active_.set(active_listeners_.size());\n  }\n  bool listenersStopped(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n    // Currently all listeners in a given direction are stopped because of the way admin\n    // drain_listener functionality is implemented. This needs to be revisited, if that changes - if\n    // we support drain by listener name,for example.\n    return stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::All ||\n           (stop_listeners_type_ == StopListenersType::InboundOnly &&\n            config.traffic_direction() == envoy::config::core::v3::INBOUND);\n  }\n  void incListenerCreateFailureStat() {\n    // TODO(mattklein123): Now that we no longer create sockets and apply options on workers,\n    // this stat is confusingly named and should be more specific, however it's unclear if it's\n    // worth changing.\n    stats_.listener_create_failure_.inc();\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Mark a listener for draining. The listener will no longer be considered active but will remain\n   * present to allow connection draining.\n   * @param listener supplies the listener to drain.\n   */\n  void drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener);\n\n  /**\n   * Start to draining filter chains that are owned by draining listener but not owned by\n   * new_listener. The new listener should have taken over the listener socket and partial of the\n   * filter chains from listener. This method is used by in place filter chain update.\n   * @param draining_listener supplies the listener to be replaced.\n   * @param new_listener supplies the new listener config which is going to replace the draining\n   * listener.\n   */\n  void drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener, ListenerImpl& new_listener);\n\n  /**\n   * Stop a listener. The listener will stop accepting new connections and its socket will be\n   * closed.\n   * @param listener supplies the listener to stop.\n   * @param options additional options to be passed through to shutdownListener.\n   * @param completion supplies the completion to be called when all workers are stopped accepting\n   * new connections. This completion is called on the main thread.\n   */\n  void stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\n                    const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\n                    std::function<void()> completion);\n\n  /**\n   * Get a listener by name. This routine is used because listeners have inherent order in static\n   * configuration and especially for tests. Thus, we can't use a map.\n   * @param listeners supplies the listener list to look in.\n   * @param name supplies the name to search for.\n   */\n  ListenerList::iterator getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name);\n\n  absl::Status setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(const std::string& name, ListenerImpl& listener);\n  absl::Status createListenSocketFactory(ListenerImpl& listener);\n\n  void maybeCloseSocketsForListener(ListenerImpl& listener);\n  absl::Status setupSocketFactoryForListener(ListenerImpl& new_listener,\n                                             const ListenerImpl& existing_listener);\n\n  ApiListenerPtr api_listener_;\n  // Active listeners are listeners that are currently accepting new connections on the workers.\n  ListenerList active_listeners_;\n  // Warming listeners are listeners that may need further initialization via the listener's init\n  // manager. For example, RDS, or in the future KDS. Once a listener is done warming it will\n  // be transitioned to active.\n  ListenerList warming_listeners_;\n  // Draining listeners are listeners that are in the process of being drained and removed. They\n  // go through two phases where first the workers stop accepting new connections and existing\n  // connections are drained. Then after that time period the listener is removed from all workers\n  // and any remaining connections are closed.\n  std::list<DrainingListener> draining_listeners_;\n  std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager> draining_filter_chains_manager_;\n\n  std::vector<WorkerPtr> workers_;\n  bool workers_started_{}\n  absl::optional<StopListenersType> stop_listeners_type_;\n  Stats::ScopeSharedPtr scope_;\n  ListenerManagerStats stats_;\n  ConfigTracker::EntryOwnerPtr config_tracker_entry_;\n  LdsApiPtr lds_api_;\n  const bool enable_dispatcher_stats_{};\n  using UpdateFailureState = envoy::admin::v3::UpdateFailureState;\n  absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, std::unique_ptr<UpdateFailureState>> error_state_tracker_;\n  FailureStates overall_error_state_;\n  Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names_;\n};\n\nclass ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder : public FilterChainFactoryBuilder {\npublic:\n  ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder(\n      ListenerImpl& listener, Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context);\n\n  absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\n  buildFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n                   FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator) const override;\n\nprivate:\n  absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr> buildFilterChainInternal(\n
